quick_facts.label: Role scope
quick_facts.value: A Fisheries Extension Officer is a fisheries-department technical and field-facing role: official Odisha materials assign the post technical files, registers for development programmes, and monthly reports/returns; the wider extension system provides technical advice, subsidy-linked developmental support, awareness meetings and training camps for fishing communities.

quick_facts.label: Core qualification route
quick_facts.value: B.F.Sc. is a direct route to this node. For example, Kerala PSC’s 2026 Fisheries Extension Officer notification accepts a bachelor’s degree in Fisheries Science; it also accepts specified postgraduate fisheries/aquaculture-related qualifications, so exact eligibility must be checked in the recruiting state’s notification.

quick_facts.label: Recruitment is state-specific
quick_facts.value: There is no single all-India recruitment rule evidenced for this title. Kerala PSC advertised the role through direct recruitment in 2026, while West Bengal’s 2024 advertisement placed Fishery Extension Officer among posts in West Bengal Junior Fishery Services Grade II. Applicants should follow the relevant State PSC or Fisheries Department notice.

quick_facts.label: Example recruitment status
quick_facts.value: As dated context only: Kerala PSC Category No. 045/2026 listed one Fisheries Extension Officer vacancy, direct recruitment, and an application deadline of August 5, 2026. Vacancy numbers, age rules, selection stages, and deadlines are notification-specific and may change.

sections.heading: What the role does
paragraphs: Fisheries Extension Officers translate fisheries-development programmes into field and administrative work. In an official Odisha Fisheries Department RTI manual, the post is assigned technical matters, maintenance of registers for development programmes, and preparation of monthly reports and returns.

The work is community-facing as well as technical. The same departmental material describes technical advice on fisheries development, including subsidy-supported activities, and public interaction through village meetings, awareness camps, and training programmes. These functions align with supporting fishers and fish farmers to adopt appropriate practices and access departmental schemes.
bullets: Handle technical matters assigned within a fisheries extension unit or office.
Maintain programme records and prepare periodic reports/returns.
Support outreach, training, awareness activities, and technical guidance connected with fisheries-development programmes.
Assist implementation and monitoring of departmental development and welfare activity; the precise remit differs by state, unit, and whether the posting is inland, aquaculture, or marine fisheries.

sections.heading: How to enter
paragraphs: The main education route for the canonical parent degree is B.F.Sc. / a bachelor’s degree in Fisheries Science. Kerala PSC’s 2026 notification explicitly lists a bachelor’s degree in Fisheries Science from a recognised university for direct recruitment to Fisheries Extension Officer. It also lists several master’s-level fisheries, aquaculture, marine-biology, aquatic-biology, fish-processing, aquaculture-engineering, and zoology qualifications as alternatives.

Recruitment commonly occurs through a State Public Service Commission or a state Fisheries Department process. In Kerala’s 2026 example, candidates apply through the PSC’s One Time Registration system; if a written, OMR, or online test is held, candidates must follow the PSC’s confirmation and admission-ticket process. A state-level fisheries technical examination syllabus in Mizoram illustrates the likely technical breadth: aquaculture, finfish and shellfish taxonomy/biology, limnology, water and soil chemistry, hatchery management, extension education, economics, engineering, fish nutrition, and fishing craft and gear.
bullets: Complete B.F.Sc. as the clearest degree route; confirm that the awarding institution and degree meet the particular notification’s recognition rules.
Track the relevant State PSC and Fisheries Department recruitment notices rather than relying on a national generic eligibility list.
Prepare for selection around fisheries science and aquaculture fundamentals, plus fisheries extension and education.
Read the complete notification for age limits, domicile/language conditions, reservations, document verification, and any test or interview requirements.

sections.heading: Skills to build
paragraphs: Technical competence should cover aquaculture and fisheries production systems, fish and shellfish biology, limnology, soil and water chemistry, hatchery management, ecology, fisheries economics, and fishing methods. These are not merely academic topics: they appear in Mizoram PSC’s technical syllabus for Fisheries Extension Officer examinations.

Extension ability is equally important. Official Odisha materials show that fisheries extension activity involves technical advice, meetings and awareness camps, training activity, record-keeping, and monthly reporting. The practical skill set therefore combines explaining recommendations clearly to diverse communities with accurate documentation and programme follow-through.
bullets: Aquaculture, hatchery, water-quality, and aquatic-ecology fundamentals.
Identification and applied knowledge of finfish and shellfish.
Fisheries extension communication, group training, and farmer/fisher engagement.
Programme documentation: registers, reports, returns, and basic monitoring data.
Working knowledge of fisheries economics, public schemes, and subsidy-linked development processes.

sections.heading: Where and how the work is carried out
paragraphs: The post can combine office administration with substantial field engagement. Odisha’s official description places Fisheries Extension Officers in marine fisheries extension units across coastal districts, supported by field-level fisheries staff, while also assigning the officer technical files, programme registers, and reports. This demonstrates the mixed field-and-office character of the work.

Posting context depends on the state and local fisheries system. A role may be connected with coastal and marine fisheries, inland aquaculture, fish-farmer development, or other departmental development programmes; candidates should treat the exact unit, geography, travel expectations, and public-contact duties as recruitment-notification-specific.
bullets: Government Fisheries Department offices and extension units.
District, coastal, block, or other field-service areas, depending on the state’s organisation.
Community meetings, awareness camps, and training venues alongside desk-based technical and reporting work.

sections.heading: Early-service development and career context
paragraphs: Career structures are state-cadre specific, so a uniform national promotion ladder should not be assumed. In Kerala’s 2026 direct-recruitment notice, a newly appointed Fisheries Extension Officer must complete two years of probation within a continuous three-year period, pass the applicable account test if not already passed, and complete the Director of Fisheries’ Staff Training Course. These requirements indicate that early progression includes administrative competence and department-specific training, not only fisheries knowledge.

For orientation rather than as a universal hierarchy, Rajasthan’s government career-guidance page depicts a pathway from Fieldman to Fish Overseer to Fisheries Extension Officer. Actual promotion beyond Fisheries Extension Officer depends on the state’s service rules, sanctioned cadre structure, seniority, examinations, and departmental orders.
bullets: Complete probation and mandatory departmental requirements where prescribed.
Build a record of sound technical advice, field implementation, documentation, and scheme delivery.
Check the applicable state service rules and Fisheries Department orders for promotion channels and higher-cadre options.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: Pay is state- and notification-dependent, so figures should be read as dated examples rather than a nationwide salary promise. Kerala PSC’s Category No. 045/2026 notification lists a scale of ₹50,200–₹1,05,300 for Fisheries Extension Officer. By comparison, West Bengal PSC’s 2024 recruitment advertisement listed Level 10 of its pay matrix, ₹32,100–₹82,900, for a combined group that included Fishery Extension Officer.

In addition to basic pay, actual monthly earnings can vary with the applicable state pay rules, dearness allowance, house-rent allowance, posting, deductions, and later pay revisions. Applicants should use the salary and pay-matrix wording in the specific advertisement that governs their appointment.
bullets: Kerala example, 2026 notification: ₹50,200–₹1,05,300 scale.
West Bengal example, 2024 notification: Level 10, ₹32,100–₹82,900.
Neither example establishes a national rate; verify the live recruiting notification and state pay rules.
